subject: Conditional Probability and Independence
question: An auto insurance company insures drivers of all ages. An actuary compiled the following statistics on the company's insured drivers: Age of Driver / Probability of Accident / Portion of Company's Insured Drivers: 16-20, 0.06, 0.08; 21-30, 0.03, 0.15; 31-65, 0.02, 0.49; 66-99, 0.04, 0.28. A randomly selected driver that the company insures has an accident. Calculate the probability that the driver was age 16-20.
